construct_verdict_reason: All items directly measure core dimensions of legal cynicism: tolerance for extrajudicial punishment, acceptance of lethal force outside due process, willingness to override judicial authority, belief in informal connections determining outcomes, and skepticism of judicial verdicts.

items.reason: Directly measures tolerance for community extrajudicial punishment when formal authorities fail, a core component of the construct.
Directly measures acceptance of lethal force by security forces as substitute for due process and judicial proceedings, a key dimension of legal cynicism.
Directly measures willingness to endorse executive override of judicial decisions, reflecting distrust of formal legal constraints on power.
Directly measures belief that informal connections (knowing the judge) rather than legal merit determine judicial outcomes, a core cynicism dimension.
Directly measures skepticism toward judicial verdicts and doubt in the legitimacy of judicial determinations of guilt, a key construct component.
